questionHtml:
At equilibrium, the concentrations of chlorine and iodine are both 0.02 mol dm–3.

12Cl2 (g) + 12I2 (g) ⇌ ICl (g)       Kc = 454

What is the concentration of iodine monochloride, ICl?


A.  4540.02

B.  454×0.02

C.  4540.04

D.  454×0.04

meta.title: At equilibrium, the concentrations of chlorine and iodine are both 0.02 mol dm–3. 12Cl2 (g) + 12I2 (g) ⇌ ICl (g) Kc = 454 What is the concentration of iodine monochloride, ICl? A.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.3—How far? The extent of chemical change

Reactivity 2.3.6—The equilibrium law is the basis for quantifying the composition of an equilibrium mixture. Solve problems involving values of K and initial and equilibrium concentrations of the components of an equilibrium mixture.
